mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly GDP growth above 2.5% and CPI inflation below 3.0% creates a soft landing environment that supports equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening corporate output capacity, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances during expansion regimes, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: High CPI inflation combined with elevated federal funds rates forces aggressive monetary tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition results in positive forward returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >58% of positive payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.4 million units signal strong residential investment demand and construction sector momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no predictive alpha after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside falling retail sales indicates weakening domestic demand and supply chain contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields positive forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over single-series baselines.
